persons.name: Mr. Brown
persons.notes: The mysterious master criminal; true identity concealed until the end

persons.name: Jane Finn
persons.notes: Sole survivor of the Lusitania disaster to have the secret documents; had amnesia

deaths.cause_detail: Poisoned by Mr. Brown to prevent her from revealing his identity to Tommy and Tuppence
deaths.motive_detail: Vandemeyer was about to expose Mr. Brown's true identity

title: N or M?
creator: Agatha Christie
notes: Second World War spy thriller; Tommy and Tuppence hunt Nazi agents in a seaside boarding house

persons.name: Tuppence Beresford
persons.notes: Independently infiltrates the boarding house

persons.name: Mrs. Sprot
persons.notes: Revealed to be a German spy; uses her own child as a shield

persons.name: Carl von Deinim
persons.notes: Suspected as a German agent; innocent

deaths.cause_detail: Shot by enemy agents after being exposed as knowing too much about the spy network at Sans Souci
deaths.motive_detail: Sheila had stumbled upon evidence of the spy ring

title: By the Pricking of My Thumbs
creator: Agatha Christie

persons.name: Tuppence Beresford
persons.notes: The main investigator in this novel

persons.name: Ada Fanshawe
persons.notes: Tommy's aunt; hinted before her death that something was wrong at the nursing home

persons.name: Mrs. Lancaster
persons.notes: Spoke cryptically about 'a child behind a fireplace'; disappeared from the nursing home

persons.name: Emma Boscowan
persons.notes: Responsible for a series of child murders; lived near the canal cottage

deaths.cause_detail: Died at the nursing home under suspicious circumstances; she had been in good health but her medication was tampered with
deaths.motive_detail: Ada had seen or heard something about the children's murders and had to be silenced

deaths.cause_detail: Multiple children were killed by Emma Boscowan and their bodies hidden near her cottage; discovered during the investigation
deaths.motive_detail: Pathological motive for child murder; details not fully elaborated in the narrative

title: Postern of Fate
creator: Agatha Christie
notes: Christie's final novel; Tommy and Tuppence's last case

persons.name: Mary Jordan
persons.notes: Killed decades ago while investigating a treasonous spy ring; her death was hidden in a children's book code

persons.name: KK
persons.notes: The long-concealed traitor responsible for Mary Jordan's death; identity uncovered at the end

deaths.cause_detail: Killed decades before the main action; her death was hidden and her murder encoded in a children's book found in the Beresfords' new home
deaths.motive_detail: Mary Jordan was investigating a spy ring and was killed to protect the traitor's identity
deaths.notes: Backstory murder that drives the entire investigation

title: And Then There Were None
creator: Agatha Christie
notes: Best-selling crime novel of all time; originally published as 'Ten Little Niggers'

persons.name: Justice Wargrave
persons.notes: The murderer; faked his own death and killed all ten guests; acted as self-appointed executioner

persons.name: Vera Claythorne
persons.notes: Let her charge Cyril Hamilton drown to clear her lover Hugo's inheritance

persons.name: Philip Lombard
persons.notes: Abandoned 21 native men in Africa to die

persons.name: Emily Brent
persons.notes: Dismissed her servant for being pregnant; the servant killed herself

persons.name: General MacArthur
persons.notes: Sent his wife's lover to certain death in WWI

deaths.cause_detail: Potassium cyanide introduced into his whisky glass at dinner on the first evening
deaths.motive_detail: Wargrave appointed himself executioner of those who had killed but escaped justice; Marston had run down two children

deaths.cause_detail: Given an overdose of sleeping medication; did not wake up
deaths.motive_detail: Mrs. Rogers and her husband had let their former employer die without calling a doctor to inherit her money

deaths.cause_detail: Struck from behind while gazing at the sea; had said he did not expect to leave the island
deaths.motive_detail: MacArthur had sent his wife's lover to certain death in WWI

deaths.cause_detail: Found dead with an axe wound to the head while chopping wood
deaths.motive_detail: Rogers and his wife let their employer die without summoning medical help

deaths.cause_detail: Injected with potassium cyanide while sitting in the kitchen; found with a bee on the table (the rhyme: 'a bumblebee stung one')
deaths.motive_detail: Emily Brent's rigidity drove her pregnant maid to suicide

deaths.cause_detail: Appeared to be shot dead in his chair; the wound was theatrical — he faked his death using a special squib and a red herring
deaths.notes: Twist: Wargrave faked his own death; he actually died last by a self-inflicted shot after killing all others

deaths.cause_detail: Thrown off the cliff into the sea by Wargrave after Armstrong had been lured into helping Wargrave fake his death
deaths.motive_detail: Armstrong had performed an operation while drunk, causing a patient's death

deaths.cause_detail: A heavy marble clock pushed from an upstairs window crushed his skull
deaths.motive_detail: Blore had given false evidence that sent an innocent man to prison, where he died

deaths.cause_detail: Shot by Vera Claythorne on the beach; she had retrieved his revolver believing him to be the killer
deaths.motive_detail: Vera believed Lombard was the last remaining killer and shot him in perceived self-defense
deaths.notes: Vera's act was technically a mistake — Lombard was not Wargrave

deaths.cause_detail: Hanged herself by the noose that Wargrave had arranged in her room; the sight of it triggered a compulsion — she walked into it almost without will
deaths.notes: Wargrave had set up the noose knowing Vera's guilt would drive her to use it; functionally a murder by manipulation

deaths.cause_detail: Shot himself in the head after writing a confession in a manuscript thrown into the sea in a bottle; died as the last and final victim of his own game
deaths.notes: The real final death; Wargrave's 'apparent' murder earlier was staged. His confession reveals the entire truth.

title: The Man in the Brown Suit
creator: Agatha Christie
notes: Early standalone adventure thriller; features Colonel Race

persons.name: Anne Beddingfeld
persons.notes: Narrator; stumbles into an international criminal conspiracy

persons.name: The Colonel
persons.notes: The 'Colonel' is the main villain — identity revealed as Harry Rayburn's father

persons.name: Nadina
persons.notes: A woman with jewels and secrets; killed at the Mill House

deaths.cause_detail: Fell or was pushed onto the London Underground track at Hyde Park Corner station; Anne Beddingfeld finds a cipher in his coat pocket
deaths.notes: Inciting incident; whether this was accident, suicide, or murder is part of the mystery

deaths.cause_detail: Strangled at the Mill House by the Colonel to prevent her from selling stolen diamonds and exposing the criminal organisation
deaths.motive_detail: Nadina possessed evidence that could expose the Colonel's criminal network

persons.name: Luke Fitzwilliam
persons.notes: Investigates after meeting an old woman on a train

persons.name: Miss Lavinia Fullerton
persons.notes: Told Luke she knew someone was committing serial murders in her village; killed before she could report to Scotland Yard

persons.name: Honoria Waynflete
persons.notes: The serial killer of Wychwood; killed multiple villagers out of obsessive passion for Gordon Easterfield

persons.name: Gordon Easterfield
persons.notes: Honoria's obsession; the indirect cause of all the murders

deaths.cause_detail: Struck down on her way to London to report the murders to Scotland Yard; her death appeared to be a traffic accident
deaths.motive_detail: Fullerton had identified Honoria as the serial killer and was about to expose her
deaths.notes: The murder that sets off Luke's investigation

deaths.cause_detail: A village boy pushed from a height; one of several suspicious deaths in Wychwood
deaths.motive_detail: The boy had seen something he should not have

deaths.cause_detail: Died of septicaemia from a wound that was secretly infected by the killer
deaths.motive_detail: Dr. Humbleby was inconvenient to Honoria's scheme

title: Death Comes as the End
creator: Agatha Christie
notes: Set in ancient Egypt circa 2000 BC; Christie's only historical mystery novel

persons.name: Renisenb
persons.notes: Narrator figure; returns home to her father's house after her husband dies

persons.name: Nofret
persons.notes: Young concubine brought home by Imhotep; her presence sets off a chain of murders

persons.name: Hori
persons.notes: The family's trusted scribe; helps Renisenb uncover the truth

persons.name: Yahmose
persons.notes: One of Imhotep's sons; revealed as the killer of multiple family members

deaths.cause_detail: Fell to her death from a cliff path; initially ruled accidental but Imhotep believes the family killed her
deaths.motive_detail: Nofret was turning Imhotep against his family and manipulating his will; the family acted to remove her
deaths.notes: Her death triggers the subsequent murders

deaths.cause_detail: Fell from the same cliff path shortly after Nofret
deaths.motive_detail: Satipy suspected the truth about Nofret's death

deaths.cause_detail: Poisoned wine at a family gathering
deaths.motive_detail: Sobek was becoming suspicious of Yahmose

title: Towards Zero
creator: Agatha Christie
notes: Features Superintendent Battle; the premise is that all events converge on a single 'zero hour' murder

persons.name: Lady Tressilian
persons.notes: Killed in her bed; golf club used as the weapon

persons.name: Nevile Strange
persons.notes: Planned the murder meticulously; arranged for suspicion to fall on others

persons.name: Audrey Strange
persons.notes: Nevile's ex-wife; framed for the murder

deaths.cause_detail: Beaten to death with a golf club (niblick) in her bedroom at night; Nevile Strange had carefully constructed a frame-up pointing to his ex-wife Audrey
deaths.motive_detail: Lady Tressilian was about to change her will and remove Nevile; he had also planned to reunite with Audrey and needed to eliminate obstacles

title: Crooked House
creator: Agatha Christie
notes: Christie's own favourite among her books; no series detective

persons.name: Charles Hayward
persons.notes: Narrator; his lover Sophia's grandfather is murdered

persons.name: Aristide Leonides
persons.notes: Self-made millionaire patriarch; insulin replaced with eserine

persons.name: Josephine Leonides
persons.notes: The killer; a highly intelligent child who killed her grandfather and Nanny Saunders

persons.name: Brenda Leonides
persons.notes: Aristide's much younger second wife; suspected along with her young lover Laurence Brown

persons.name: Nanny Saunders
persons.notes: Killed by Josephine to silence her

deaths.cause_detail: His insulin was replaced with eserine (physostigmine sulphate), causing death from the injected dose; appeared as an accidental insulin overdose
deaths.motive_detail: Josephine felt overlooked and not special; killed from a mixture of excitement at committing a 'perfect crime' and resentment at not being the centre of attention
deaths.notes: One of Christie's most shocking twists: the killer is an 11-year-old girl

deaths.cause_detail: Arsenic introduced into her cocoa; Nanny had begun to suspect the truth about Aristide's murder
deaths.motive_detail: Nanny Saunders was on the verge of realising that Josephine had killed her grandfather

deaths.cause_detail: Died in a car accident when her grandmother Edith de Haviland drove her off the road; Edith chose this end to prevent Josephine from standing trial
deaths.motive_detail: Edith, the family's moral centre, chose to end Josephine's life rather than see the child tried for murder and ruin the family
deaths.notes: Edith de Haviland also died in the car crash; a private execution and suicide combined

title: Ordeal by Innocence
creator: Agatha Christie
notes: Christie called this one of her personal favourites; no series detective

persons.name: Arthur Calgary
persons.notes: Could have provided an alibi for Jacko Argyle but was away on expedition; returns to clear his name

persons.name: Rachel Argyle
persons.notes: Adoptive mother of five children; murdered by one of them

persons.name: Jacko Argyle
persons.notes: Convicted of Rachel's murder and died in prison; posthumously cleared

persons.name: Hester Argyle
persons.notes: The actual killer; could not bear her adoptive mother's suffocating dominance

deaths.cause_detail: Struck over the head with a poker in her study
deaths.motive_detail: Hester could not tolerate Rachel's controlling, suffocating hold over the family; killed her in a desperate act of rebellion
deaths.notes: Twist: Jacko was convicted and died for a crime he did not commit; Calgary's return triggers the real killer's exposure

deaths.cause_detail: Died in prison while serving his sentence for a murder he did not commit
deaths.notes: Backstory; a miscarriage of justice that haunts the novel

title: The Pale Horse
creator: Agatha Christie
notes: Features Ariadne Oliver; the thallium poisoning is now famous for real-world forensic cases it inspired

persons.name: Ariadne Oliver
persons.notes: Christie's self-parody character

persons.name: Zachariah Osborne
persons.notes: The real killer behind the 'witchcraft' operation; supplies thallium to a murder-for-hire network

persons.name: Thyrzta Grey
persons.notes: Runs The Pale Horse as an occult cover for a murder-for-hire scheme

deaths.cause_detail: Struck on the head in the street after hearing a dying woman's confession that contained the names of murder targets
deaths.motive_detail: Gorman had written down the names of the targets; the network needed those names destroyed

deaths.cause_detail: Multiple people across England died from thallium poisoning, administered by Osborne on behalf of paying clients through the Pale Horse network
deaths.motive_detail: Osborne ran a murder-for-hire business; clients paid to have relatives or enemies killed
deaths.notes: Twist: the occult practitioners at The Pale Horse are a deliberate red herring; the actual killing mechanism is thallium poisoning by the pharmacist Osborne

title: Endless Night
creator: Agatha Christie
notes: Stylistically different from Christie's other work; written as a psychological thriller with an unreliable narrator

persons.name: Michael Rogers
persons.notes: The narrator and killer; in love with Gipsy's Acre and conspired to inherit the land and fortune

persons.name: Ellie Rogers
persons.notes: Rich American heiress who married Michael; he married her to get her fortune, then had her killed

persons.name: Greta Andersen
persons.notes: Michael's true lover; conspired with him to kill Ellie

deaths.cause_detail: Killed through an induced allergic reaction by Greta Andersen; Michael and Greta conspired to kill Ellie so Michael could inherit her fortune and be free to live with Greta at Gipsy's Acre
deaths.motive_detail: Michael and Greta planned to kill Ellie so Michael would inherit; they had planned this even before Michael's marriage to Ellie
deaths.notes: Twist: the narrator Michael is revealed in the final pages to have been the architect of his wife's murder from the start; the apparent love story is exposed as premeditated murder

deaths.cause_detail: Killed by Michael after she began to become a liability
deaths.motive_detail: Greta knew everything and had become a danger to Michael
